title: Hypertable chunking model
description: Time-series engines partition data into time-bounded chunks so writes hit recent chunks and queries prune by time.
body: Use when reasoning about why time-series query performance depends on time predicates. A hypertable is transparently partitioned into chunks by a time interval (and optionally a space dimension); inserts target the chunk for their timestamp and queries with a time predicate skip non-matching chunks. Do not treat it as one flat table — without a time filter the planner cannot prune. Actions: always bound queries by time and size chunks to the working set. Validation signals include EXPLAIN chunk exclusion. Risk boundary: chunk count and interval directly affect both write and plan cost.
